project.name: Scout Robot
project.description: Hobby autonomous ground robot. Jetson Orin Nano main compute, Pi 4 ROS2 bridge, Pico micro-ROS I/O. 12V LiFePO4 power, RTK GPS, depth cameras, closed-loop steering.
project.notes: This manifest is the single source of truth for Scout wiring. AI maintains it. Do not hand-edit generated docs.

12v_main.notes: Protected 12V rail from BP-65 output. All loads except Jetson (which may run direct from bus bar).

5v_logic.notes: 5V logic rail for Pi4, Pico (VSYS), relay board.

3v3_pico.notes: 3.3V from Pico 3V3_OUT. Powers AS5600 encoders only.

jetson.name: Jetson Orin Nano 8GB
jetson.description: Main compute. Runs ROS2, perception stack. Powers directly from 12V bus.
jetson.notes: Power input: 9-20V DC barrel jack. Ethernet to UniFi switch.

pi4.name: Raspberry Pi 4 8GB
pi4.description: ROS2 bridge. Manages GPS corrections, Pico reset, hazcam pipeline. Connects to UniFi switch and 7-port USB hub.
pi4.notes: Powered via USB-C 5V. Ethernet to UniFi switch. GPIO pin used to reset Pico.

gpio_pico_reset.notes: Drive LOW to reset Pico. Use 1kΩ series resistor.

pico.name: Raspberry Pi Pico (micro-ROS)
pico.description: Low-level I/O controller. Runs micro-ROS over USB serial to Pi4. Controls encoders, relay board, headlights, signal tower, RC input.
pico.notes: Powered via VSYS from 5V step-down. USB to Pi4 for micro-ROS. RUN pin wired to Pi4 GPIO for automated flashing.

GP9.function: UART1 RX — iBUS from RC receiver

RUN.notes: Drive LOW to reset. 1kΩ series resistor from Pi4 GPIO.

pololu_24v12.name: Pololu Simple High-Power Motor Controller 24v12
pololu_24v12.description: Drive motor controller. USB control from Pi4 via 7-port hub. RC input direct from receiver for hardware E-stop.

rc_in.notes: Direct from FS-iA10B receiver. Hardware stop independent of software.

pololu_jrk_g2.name: Pololu Jrk G2 21v3
pololu_jrk_g2.description: Steering motor controller. Closed-loop position control via feedback potentiometer. USB control from Pi4.
pololu_jrk_g2.notes: USB: /dev/serial/by-id/usb-Pololu_Corporation_Pololu_Jrk_G2_21v3_* (two interfaces: command + feedback). I2C address configurable.

feedback.notes: 0–5V analog from linear pot. Jrk uses this for closed-loop position control.

battery.name: 12V 100Ah LiFePO4 Battery
battery.description: Main power source. Built-in 100A BMS. 12V nominal.
battery.notes: Connected via 10AWG XT60 with physical disconnect. 40A inline fuse on positive lead.

inline_fuse_40a.name: 40A Inline Fuse
inline_fuse_40a.description: Overcurrent protection on battery positive lead.

xt60_disconnect.name: XT60 Battery Disconnect
xt60_disconnect.description: Physical battery disconnect. 10AWG XT60 connector pair allows battery removal without tools.
xt60_disconnect.notes: Male connector on battery side, female on harness side.

bus_bar.name: Bussmann Stud-Type Bus Bar
bus_bar.description: Main positive and negative distribution point. Ring terminals bolted to each stud. Two studs: positive and negative.
bus_bar.notes: Torque fasteners per Bussmann spec label. Positive stud feeds BP-65 and Jetson 12V. Negative stud is common chassis ground.

bp65.name: Victron BatteryProtect BP-65
bp65.description: Primary load disconnect. Protects battery from over-discharge. H/L pin is E-stop signal input.
bp65.notes: H/L remote pin: connect to B- to disconnect loads. Normally open-circuit (loads connected). E-stop wiring TBD — not yet wired.

hl_pin.notes: Connect to B- to disconnect loads (turn off). Normally floating = loads on. E-stop N.C. contact wiring TBD.

battery_sense.name: Victron Smart Battery Sense
battery_sense.description: Wireless voltage and temperature sensor. Connects to battery terminals. Reports via Bluetooth to Victron app.
battery_sense.notes: No wired data connection to compute stack. Bluetooth only.

step_down_5v.name: 5V/10A DC-DC Step-Down Converter
step_down_5v.description: Converts 12V to 5V/10A for Pi4, Pico (VSYS), and relay board.

charger.name: Victron Blue Smart Charger IP65 12V/15A
charger.description: In-place shore power battery charger. 120VAC in, 12V/15A out. Bluetooth monitoring.
charger.notes: Connected to NOCO GCP1 AC inlet. Charges battery directly while robot is stationary.

shore_inlet.name: NOCO GCP1 15A AC Inlet
shore_inlet.description: NEMA 5-15R waterproof shore power inlet. Mounts to chassis. Feeds charger.

usb_charger.name: Coolgear 75W Dual USB Charger (USB-A + USB-C PD)
usb_charger.description: Field laptop/device charging. 12V input, USB-A and USB-C PD output. QC4.0/PPS support.

unifi_switch.name: Ubiquiti UniFi USW Flex Mini (5-Port GbE)
unifi_switch.description: Managed gigabit switch. Distributes network to Jetson, Pi4, and UniFi AP.
unifi_switch.notes: Powered via PoE from AP or 5V USB-C. Connect 5V if not using PoE.

unifi_ap.name: Ubiquiti UAP-AC-M-US UniFi AC Mesh AP
unifi_ap.description: Primary WiFi uplink. Mesh capable. Connected to UniFi switch port 1.

usb_hub.name: 7-Port USB 3.0 Hub (powered)
usb_hub.description: Powered USB 3.0 hub connected to Pi4. Distributes USB to hazcams, motor controllers, and DSO-2090.
usb_hub.notes: Hub-powered. Connected to Pi4 USB-A port. Motor controllers and hazcams share this hub.

oak_d.name: OAK-D Depth Camera
oak_d.description: Stereo depth camera with built-in IMU. USB 3.0 to Pi4 direct (not via hub — bandwidth).

logitech_c910.name: Logitech HD Webcam C910
logitech_c910.description: General purpose camera. USB to Jetson.

clearview.name: ClearVIEW HD-USB
clearview.description: FPV video system. Connection mode TBD: USB camera or IP stream.
clearview.notes: TBD: decide USB vs IP mode before wiring.

as5600_left.name: AS5600 Magnetic Encoder (Left Drive Motor)
as5600_left.description: 12-bit magnetic rotary position encoder on left drive motor. I2C address 0x36 on I2C0.

SDA.notes: I2C address 0x36. 4.7kΩ pull-up to 3.3V required on bus.

as5600_right.name: AS5600 Magnetic Encoder (Right Drive Motor)
as5600_right.description: 12-bit magnetic rotary position encoder on right drive motor. I2C address 0x36 on I2C1 (separate bus from left).

SDA.notes: I2C address 0x36. Separate bus from left encoder. 4.7kΩ pull-up to 3.3V required.

steering_pot.name: Linear Sliding Potentiometer (Steering Feedback)
steering_pot.description: Analog position feedback for steering. Wiper output feeds Jrk G2 analog feedback input for closed-loop control.

wiper.notes: Wired directly to Jrk G2 feedback input.

rtk_rover_1.name: simpleRTK2B Lite (Rover — Primary/Heading)
rtk_rover_1.description: RTK GPS rover module. One of two mounted on Scout for moving baseline heading. Connects to Pi4 via FTDI USB-serial.
rtk_rover_1.notes: Moving baseline pair with rtk_rover_2. Provides centimeter-level heading.

rtk_rover_2.name: simpleRTK2B Lite (Rover — Secondary/Position)
rtk_rover_2.description: Second RTK GPS rover module. Moving baseline pair with rtk_rover_1 for heading + absolute position. Connects to Pi4 via FTDI.

rc_receiver.name: Flysky FS-iA10B RC Receiver
rc_receiver.description: 10-channel 2.4GHz AFHDS 2A receiver. iBUS output to Pico. One PWM channel wired direct to Pololu 24v12 as hardware E-stop.
rc_receiver.notes: Powered from 5V. iBUS is single-wire serial at 115200 baud.

pwm_ch6.function: PWM E-stop channel → Pololu 24v12 RC input
pwm_ch6.notes: Hardware E-stop. Direct to motor controller, bypasses software entirely.

transistor_board.name: Custom Transistor Board
transistor_board.description: Custom PCB using transistors to switch 12V loads from Pico 3.3V PWM signals. Powers headlights and signal tower.
transistor_board.notes: 5 channels. Input: 3.3V PWM from Pico. Output: 12V switched to load. Each channel is PWM-capable.

relay_board.name: 8-Channel Relay Module (Active-Low, 5V)
relay_board.description: 8-channel 5V relay board. Active-low logic (Pico GPIO LOW = relay energized). Loads TBD.
relay_board.notes: VCC: 5V. Logic input: active-low. Pico outputs 3.3V — verify optocoupler triggers at 3.3V before final wiring. Load assignments TBD.

grove_led_left.name: Grove Variable Color LED V1.1 (Headlight Left)
grove_led_left.description: Left headlight. 12V input via transistor board channel 1.

grove_led_right.name: Grove Variable Color LED V1.1 (Headlight Right)
grove_led_right.description: Right headlight. 12V input via transistor board channel 2.

signal_tower.name: LED Signal Tower (2-Layer + Buzzer, 12V)
signal_tower.description: Industrial signal tower. 2 light layers + buzzer. 12V. Steady/flash switchable. Powered via transistor board channels 3–5.

estop_button.name: GCX1131 E-Stop Pushbutton (22mm, Twist-to-Release)
estop_button.description: Red mushroom 40mm twist-to-release E-stop. N.C. contact. Mounted in enclosure. Wired to BP-65 H/L signal input. NOT YET WIRED.
estop_button.notes: N.C. contact: closed during normal operation, opens when pressed. Wiring to BP-65 H/L TBD — circuit design needed.

dso_2090.name: DSO-2090 USB Oscilloscope
dso_2090.description: 2-channel USB oscilloscope. Monitors 5V and 12V power rails as ROS topics. Also used during assembly for signal verification. USB2, powered from hub.
dso_2090.notes: Connected via 7-port hub. Hub provides power. Probe CH1 on 12V rail, CH2 on 5V rail.

commissioning.notes: Measure fuse output. Expect ~12V with battery connected.
Measure bus bar positive stud.
Measure Jetson DC barrel jack before connecting.
Measure Pi4 USB-C power pins. Expect 5V ± 0.25V.
Measure Pico VSYS (pin 39).
Measure at encoder VCC pad with Pico powered.
Scan I2C0 bus (GP4/GP5). Expect AS5600 at 0x36.
Verified as part of I2C scan in enc_left_sda test.
Scan I2C1 bus (GP6/GP7). Expect AS5600 at 0x36.
Verify iBUS packets received on UART1. Power transmitter, check Pico receives channel data.

connections.label: RC_ESTOP_DIRECT
connections.notes: Hardware E-stop. RC ch6 low = motor stop. Independent of software stack.

commissioning.notes: Verify by commanding E-stop from transmitter and confirming motor controller stops.
Drive GP10 LOW, relay 1 should click and LED illuminate.
Measure transistor board ch1_in with DSO-2090. Drive GP18 PWM, verify signal present.
With GP18 driven HIGH, measure LED supply. Expect ~12V.
Drive GP22 HIGH, buzzer should sound.

wire.notes: 1kΩ series resistor inline on this wire.

commissioning.notes: Pi4 drives GPIO17 LOW, Pico should reset (USB disconnect/reconnect observable).
Verify Pico appears as /dev/ttyACM* on Pi4. Run micro-ROS agent and confirm node appears.
Verify hub appears on Pi4: lsusb should show VIA Labs hub.
lsusb: expect Pololu Simple High-Power Motor Controller 24v12
lsusb: expect Pololu Jrk G2 21v3 (two ACM interfaces)
lsusb: expect DSO-2090. Verify power rail monitoring ROS topic publishes.

wire.notes: USB 3.0 direct to Pi4, not via hub — bandwidth requirement.

commissioning.notes: Verify OAK-D publishes depth and IMU topics.
Ping Pi4 from Jetson to verify switch routing.
Verify WiFi SSID visible and devices can connect.
Move steering through full range. Voltage should sweep 0–5V smoothly.

wire.notes: 14AWG 3-conductor AC cable. Shore power only — disconnect before operating robot.

commissioning.notes: Test with shore power connected: verify charger LED shows bulk/absorption charge mode.

bom_overrides.description: 1kΩ resistor — Pico RUN reset line (Pi4 GPIO17 → Pico RUN)
